Add smart caching: permanent response cache + targeted query cache

Add white-label callout below pricing tiers (#109)

* fix: terms link on signup uses in-app navigation instead of /terms URL

The SPA has no server route for /terms, so window.open('/terms') 404'd.
Changed to use onShowTerms callback that navigates within the app.
Back button on terms page returns to signup form (not landing page).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent password setup modal from popping twice for invited users

supabase.auth.updateUser() triggers a USER_UPDATED event that re-fetches
the profile. During the brief re-fetch, the stale profile (without
password_set) caused the useEffect to fire again, reopening the modal.

Added passwordSetDone ref that locks out the effect after the first
successful password save in the session.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(foundation): impute 2016 age brackets via population-scaled 2021 baseline

Age bracket columns (pop_0_to_14/15_24/25_64/65_plus) were NULL for all 158
2016 rows because the census_extended source only covers 2021.

Fix: add census_extended_2021_baseline CTE and scale the 2021 counts by the
2016/2021 population ratio for each neighbourhood. Consistent with the existing
imputation pattern used for income, education, and dwelling value. is_imputed
flag already marks all 2016 rows as estimated.

Result: 0 nulls across 316 rows in mart_neighbourhood_people.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve autoplay success rate and reduce token cost

- Lower keyword match threshold 55% → 30% (guesser paraphrases, exact
  word matches were impossible at 55%)
- Instruct story generator to use simple everyday words in full_answer
  so guesser's natural phrasing overlaps with scoring keywords
- Guide solution prompt to reuse words from Q&A clues, improving match rate
- Drop story max_tokens 4096 → 1024 (short 2-paragraph story needs far less)

Together targets ~40% solve rate vs near-0% previously.

- Throttled path uses emit_hook_silent to prevent clobbering output on /resume
- Success message now includes summary of what bootstrap actually did
- Plugin update errors captured with exit code and reason instead of "skipped"
- Removed bootstrap from its own plugin update list to avoid self-update loop
- Bump plugin version to 0.2.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: truncate tool results, error disclosure, thinking display (#57)

* fix: truncate tool results for LLM context, error disclosure, thinking display

Truncate large tool results (e.g. corpus_overview with 233 docs) to 24K
chars before sending to the LLM, preventing 400 errors from context
overflow. Full results are still saved to DB. Error events now include
the response body in a collapsible detail section. Models that emit
<think> blocks (Qwen3, DeepSeek R1) get a collapsible Reasoning section
instead of raw tags.

  response to actually capture error details

commit.message:
Add gh-tool, mise-tool, and permissions-sync plugins (#206)

* feat: add mise-tool, gh-tool, and permissions-sync plugins

Three new plugins for Claude Code session management:

- mise-tool: Auto-installs mise to $project/bin/.local on web sessions,
  provides comprehensive mise workflow skills
- gh-tool: Auto-installs GitHub CLI to $project/bin/.local on web sessions,
  provides comprehensive gh workflow skills
- permissions-sync: Merges permission scopes from configurable sources
  (including GitHub repos like nsheaps/cept) into settings.local.json

All three use the project-local binary pattern (bin/.local/ gitignored,
added to PATH via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE) and support configuration via
plugins.settings.yaml at project or user level.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CD6235Q5sy4tSUBeWr2uc8

* chore: update memory with new plugin entities

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CD6235Q5sy4tSUBeWr2uc8

* chore: `just lint`

* feat: add sequential-thinking plugin with MCP server and auto-permissions

Sets up the @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking MCP server
via .mcp.json and auto-adds mcp__sequential-thinking__* to permissions.allow
in settings.local.json on session start.

Includes comprehensive skill documentation for structured problem-solving
patterns (linear analysis, revision, branching, adaptive depth).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CD6235Q5sy4tSUBeWr2uc8

* chore: `just lint`

* refactor: extract shared libs for plugin hooks (DRY)

Create shared/lib/ with reusable bash libraries:
- plugin-config-read.sh: 3-tier YAML config resolution
- tool-install.sh: project-local binary installation helpers
- add-permission.sh: idempotent permission injection

Refactor all 5 plugin hook scripts to use shared libs instead of
duplicated inline config/install functions. Add .claude/rules/shared-libs.md
documenting the patterns and conventions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CD6235Q5sy4tSUBeWr2uc8

* chore: `just lint`

* fix: revert marketplace.json, fix CI check for new plugins, add CI-must-pass rule

- Revert marketplace.json to main (auto-generated by CD on merge)
- Update check-version-files to allow new plugin.json files while still
  blocking modifications to existing ones (--diff-filter=M)
- Add "CI Must Pass" rule to conventions with GH_TOKEN usage instructions

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CD6235Q5sy4tSUBeWr2uc8

* Change source path from directory to GitHub

commit.message:
fix: pass req to all payload calls within admission approval transaction

When processAdmissionApproval creates a student then enrollment within
the same transaction, hook functions (Enrollment title beforeChange,
syncStudentStatus afterChange, student track afterChange) were calling
payload.findByID/update without req. This caused them to run outside
the transaction and fail to see the just-created student/user records.

- Enrollment title beforeChange: pass req to findByID for student+batch
- syncStudentStatus: pass req to findByID and update; guard student null
- track.ts: pass req to findByID for user email lookup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Claude Code skills and fizz install-skills command (#324)

Adds four AI assistant skills for FizzBee (fizz-spec, fizz-check,
fizz-debug, fizz-mbt) that work across Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI,
and other tools supporting the Agent Skills standard.

- .claude/skills/: skill files covering spec writing, model checking,
  debugging, and model-based testing (TypeScript/Go/Rust/Java)
- install-claude-skills.sh: standalone bootstrap script (curl | bash)
  that installs skills + reference docs + curated examples to ~/.claude/skills/
- fizz install-skills: new subcommand in the fizz wrapper that does the
  same, intended as the primary post-install update command
- .github/instructions/: updated fizzbee.instructions.md (was a stub)
  and fizzbee-mbt.instructions.md (now covers all four languages with
  TypeScript/Playwright best practices)
- README: documents the skills feature and update workflow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable -resume for all 7 pipeline launches

Nextflow's -resume flag reuses cached task outputs from previous runs
with the same session ID / work directory. This avoids re-running
completed tasks when a pipeline is restarted after a failure, saving
significant compute time on long-running steps (sarek ~8h, rnaseq ~4h).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: raise SWIPE_MIN_PEAK_VELOCITY 0.03 -> 0.10

Chin-drop false positives peak at 0.03-0.07; real swipes at 0.15-0.22.
0.10 sits in the gap and kills all chin-drop fires with margin to spare.
x-ratio gate already at 0.40 everywhere, no 0.60 remnants.

commit.message:
feat: Tika CLI improvements — metadata-only, quiet output, zio-logging (#69)

* fix(info): use metadata-only Tika extraction in CLI info command

Switch extractMetadata() from BodyContentHandler(-1) (full body parse)
to DocumentInfo.extractMetadataOnly() which uses WriteOutContentHandler(0)
to bail on the first body character. Same metadata, much faster on large
or scanned documents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(aspose): decouple license init from capability queries

BackendWiring.asposeCanConvert/asposeCanSplit now call static format
lookups (AsposeTransforms.canConvert/canSplit) instead of the licensed
variants that trigger AsposeLicenseV2.isProductLicensed() side effects.

This eliminates eager Aspose license initialization on every `xlcr info`,
server GET /capabilities (442+ canConvert calls), POST /info, and
POST /convert pre-flight checks. The actual license gate still fires at
conversion time — unlicensed products produce ResourceError caught by
the fallback chain in UnifiedTransforms.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): route logback to stderr, add env-based log control

- Console appender now targets System.err instead of System.out, keeping
  stdout clean for structured CLI output (JSON, XML, converted bytes)
- XLCR_LOG_LEVEL env var controls root log level (default: INFO)
- XLCR_LOG_FILE env var controls log file path (default: logs/application.log,
  set to /dev/null to disable for ephemeral containers like Modal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): default-quiet output, gate success messages behind --verbose

Move "Successfully converted/split" messages behind --verbose flag.
By default the CLI now produces no stdout noise — only the converted
file or structured info output. Standard CLI convention: quiet by
default, -v opts into progress messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): replace logback with zio-logging + SLF4J2 bridge

Replace logback-classic with zio-logging and zio-logging-slf4j2-bridge
for unified, ZIO-native logging:

- All ZIO.log* calls route through ZIO's console-err logger (stderr)
- Third-party SLF4J calls (Tika, POI, Aspose, JODConverter) are captured
  by the SLF4J2 bridge and routed through the same ZIO logger
- Log4j2 calls (Tika) still bridge via log4j-to-slf4j -> ZIO
- XLCR_LOG_LEVEL env var controls root level (default: WARN)
- stdout is now completely clean for structured output (JSON, XML, bytes)
- Remove logback.xml — no longer needed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cli): remove redundant shell backend-info from wrapper

The wrapper script had its own shell-based --backend-info handler that
duplicated (and disagreed with) the Java CLI's output. It couldn't
detect bundled Aspose licenses and reported "Evaluation mode" even when
the license was properly loaded at runtime.

Remove the shell check entirely — the Java CLI's --backend-info has
accurate runtime license detection and backend status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(cli): add opt-in --license-aware-capabilities flag

Thread licenseAwareCapabilities through BackendWiring, UnifiedTransforms,
all server routes, and CLI info/server commands. Default remains fast
static lookups (no Aspose license init). Opt-in via:

- CLI: `xlcr info -i doc.pdf --license-aware-capabilities`
- Server: `xlcr server start --license-aware-capabilities`
- Env: `XLCR_LICENSE_AWARE_CAPABILITIES=1`

Server capabilities cache stores both modes lazily. Tests added for
CLI flag parsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
